---
title: "PCA_BULK_RNA_SEQ_ANALYSIS"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
# ============================================================
# PCA Plot — All Samples + Per Genotype Group
# ============================================================

library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(patchwork)

# ============================================================
# OUTPUT PATHS
# ============================================================

output_dir  <- "/Users/preddy/Desktop/Lab_Streeter_new/BULKRNASEQ_AJ/PCA_RESULTS"
output_dir2 <- "/Users/preddy/Desktop/Lab_Streeter_new/BULKRNASEQ_AJ/PCA_PER_GENOTYPE"
dir.create(output_dir,  showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(output_dir2, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ============================================================
# STEP 1: Load count matrix
# ============================================================

se         <- readRDS(
  "/Users/preddy/Desktop/Lab_Streeter_new/BULKRNASEQ_AJ/salmon.merged.gene.SummarizedExperiment.rds"
)

# KEY FIX: as.matrix() first, then round, then force integer
counts_mat <- as.matrix(assay(se, "counts"))
counts_mat <- round(counts_mat)
mode(counts_mat) <- "integer"

gene_names <- rowData(se)$gene_name

# Verify it's a proper integer matrix
cat("Class:", class(counts_mat), "\n")
cat("Mode:",  mode(counts_mat),  "\n")
cat("Dimensions:", dim(counts_mat), "\n")

# Rename samples
colnames(counts_mat)[colnames(counts_mat) == "A1"] <- "AO_WT_HFD_A1"
colnames(counts_mat)[colnames(counts_mat) == "D1"] <- "AO_WT_HFD_D1"
colnames(counts_mat)[colnames(counts_mat) == "G1"] <- "AO_KO_HFD_G1"

cat("Checking renamed samples:\n")
print(colnames(counts_mat)[grep("A1|D1|B1|G1", colnames(counts_mat))])

# ============================================================
# STEP 2: Define ALL samples metadata
# ============================================================

sample_info_all <- data.frame(
  sample_id = c(
    # OPA1 WT CD
    "OPA1_WT_CD_1", "OPA1_WT_CD_2", "OPA1_WT_CD_3",
    # OPA1 WT HFD
    "OPA1_WT_HFD_4", "OPA1_WT_HFD_5", "OPA1_WT_HFD_6",
    # OPA1 KO CD
    "OPA1_KO_CD_7", "OPA1_KO_CD_8", "OPA1_KO_CD_9",
    "OPA1_KO_CD_10", "OPA1_KO_CD_11", "OPA1_KO_CD_F1",
    # OPA1 KO HFD
    "OPA1_KO_HFD_12", "OPA1_KO_HFD_C1", "OPA1_KO_HFD_E1",
    # ATF4 WT CD
    "ATF4_WT_CD_13", "ATF4_WT_CD_14", "ATF4_WT_CD_18",
    # ATF4 WT HFD
    "ATF4_WT_HFD_15", "ATF4_WT_HFD_16", "ATF4_WT_HFD_17",
    # ATF4 KO CD
    "ATF4_KO_CD_31", "ATF4_KO_CD_32", "ATF4_KO_CD_36",
    # ATF4 KO HFD
    "ATF4_KO_HFD_33", "ATF4_KO_HFD_34", "ATF4_KO_HFD_35",
    # AO WT CD
    "AO_WT_CD_19", "AO_WT_CD_20", "AO_WT_CD_21",
    # AO WT HFD
    "AO_WT_HFD_22", "AO_WT_HFD_23", "AO_WT_HFD_24",
    "AO_WT_HFD_A1", "AO_WT_HFD_D1",
    # AO KO CD
    "AO_KO_CD_25", "AO_KO_CD_26", "AO_KO_CD_B1",
    # AO KO HFD
    "AO_KO_HFD_27", "AO_KO_HFD_28", "AO_KO_HFD_29", "AO_KO_HFD_30",
    "AO_KO_HFD_G1"
  ),
  genotype_gene = c(
    rep("OPA1", 15),
    rep("ATF4", 12),
    rep("AO",   16)
  ),
  KO_status = c(
    rep("WT", 6),  rep("KO", 9),
    rep("WT", 6),  rep("KO", 6),
    rep("WT", 8),  rep("KO", 8)
  ),
  diet = c(
    rep("CD", 3), rep("HFD", 3), rep("CD", 6), rep("HFD", 3),
    rep("CD", 3), rep("HFD", 3), rep("CD", 3), rep("HFD", 3),
    rep("CD", 3), rep("HFD", 5), rep("CD", 3), rep("HFD", 5)
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

rownames(sample_info_all) <- sample_info_all$sample_id

sample_info_all <- sample_info_all %>%
  mutate(group = paste(genotype_gene, KO_status, sep = "_"))

cat("Total samples:", nrow(sample_info_all), "\n")
print(table(sample_info_all$group))

# ============================================================
# STEP 3: Build DESeq2 object for ALL samples
# ============================================================

all_counts <- counts_mat[, sample_info_all$sample_id]

coldata_all <- sample_info_all
coldata_all$KO_status     <- factor(coldata_all$KO_status,     levels = c("WT", "KO"))
coldata_all$diet          <- factor(coldata_all$diet,          levels = c("CD", "HFD"))
coldata_all$genotype_gene <- factor(coldata_all$genotype_gene, levels = c("OPA1", "ATF4", "AO"))
coldata_all$group         <- factor(coldata_all$group)

dds_all <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = all_counts,
  colData   = coldata_all,
  design    = ~ group
)

dds_all <- dds_all[rowSums(counts(dds_all) >= 10) >= 3, ]
vst_all <- vst(dds_all, blind = TRUE)
cat("Genes after filtering:", nrow(vst_all), "\n")

# ============================================================
# STEP 4: PCA — All Samples
# ============================================================

pca_data <- plotPCA(vst_all,
                    intgroup   = c("genotype_gene", "KO_status", "diet"),
                    returnData = TRUE,
                    ntop       = 500)

percent_var <- round(100 * attr(pca_data, "percentVar"))
cat("PC1:", percent_var[1], "% | PC2:", percent_var[2], "%\n")

pca_data <- pca_data %>%
  mutate(group     = paste(genotype_gene, KO_status, sep = "_"),
         sample_id = rownames(.))

# ============================================================
# STEP 5: Color and shape scheme
# ============================================================

group_colors <- c(
  "OPA1_WT" = "#2166AC",
  "OPA1_KO" = "#92C5DE",
  "ATF4_WT" = "#1A9641",
  "ATF4_KO" = "#A6D96A",
  "AO_WT"   = "#D73027",
  "AO_KO"   = "#FDAE61"
)
diet_shapes <- c("CD" = 16, "HFD" = 17)

# ============================================================
# STEP 6: Plot 1 — All samples by Genotype Group
# ============================================================

p_pca1 <- ggplot(pca_data,
                 aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                     color = group, shape = diet, label = sample_id)) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = group), level = 0.75,
               linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2, max.overlaps = 20,
                  box.padding = 0.3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = group_colors, name = "Genotype_Status") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = diet_shapes,  name = "Diet") +
  labs(
    title    = "PCA — All Samples",
    subtitle = paste0("Top 500 variable genes | PC1: ", percent_var[1],
                      "% | PC2: ", percent_var[2], "%"),
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percent_var[1], "% variance)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percent_var[2], "% variance)")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
        legend.position = "right")

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_group.pdf"), p_pca1, width = 10, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_group.png"), p_pca1, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 150)
cat("Plot 1 saved\n")

# ============================================================
# STEP 7: Plot 2 — by KO Status
# ============================================================

p_pca2 <- ggplot(pca_data,
                 aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                     color = KO_status, shape = genotype_gene, label = sample_id)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2, max.overlaps = 20,
                  box.padding = 0.3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("WT" = "#2166AC", "KO" = "#D73027"), name = "KO Status") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("OPA1" = 16, "ATF4" = 17, "AO" = 15), name = "Genotype") +
  labs(
    title    = "PCA — All Samples (by KO Status)",
    subtitle = paste0("Top 500 variable genes | PC1: ", percent_var[1],
                      "% | PC2: ", percent_var[2], "%"),
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percent_var[1], "% variance)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percent_var[2], "% variance)")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
        legend.position = "right")

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_KO.pdf"), p_pca2, width = 10, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_KO.png"), p_pca2, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 150)
cat("Plot 2 saved\n")

# ============================================================
# STEP 8: Plot 3 — by Diet
# ============================================================

p_pca3 <- ggplot(pca_data,
                 aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                     color = diet, shape = genotype_gene, label = sample_id)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2, max.overlaps = 20,
                  box.padding = 0.3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("CD" = "black", "HFD" = "orange"), name = "Diet") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("OPA1" = 16, "ATF4" = 17, "AO" = 15), name = "Genotype") +
  labs(
    title    = "PCA — All Samples (by Diet)",
    subtitle = paste0("Top 500 variable genes | PC1: ", percent_var[1],
                      "% | PC2: ", percent_var[2], "%"),
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percent_var[1], "% variance)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percent_var[2], "% variance)")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
        legend.position = "right")

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_diet.pdf"), p_pca3, width = 10, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_diet.png"), p_pca3, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 150)
cat("Plot 3 saved\n")

# ============================================================
# STEP 9: Save PCA coordinates
# ============================================================

write.csv(
  pca_data %>% select(sample_id, genotype_gene, KO_status, diet, PC1, PC2),
  file.path(output_dir, "PCA_coordinates.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)
cat("PCA coordinates saved\n")

# ============================================================
# STEP 10: PCA per Genotype (OPA1, ATF4, AO)
# Fresh DESeq2 + VST per group — variance within group only
# ============================================================

make_pca_per_group <- function(counts_mat, sample_info_all, genotype, color_wt, color_ko) {
  
  meta <- sample_info_all %>% filter(genotype_gene == genotype)
  
  # KEY FIX: as.matrix + mode conversion INSIDE function
  cts <- as.matrix(counts_mat[, meta$sample_id])
  cts <- round(cts)
  mode(cts) <- "integer"
  
  coldata <- meta
  coldata$KO_status <- factor(coldata$KO_status, levels = c("WT", "KO"))
  coldata$diet      <- factor(coldata$diet,      levels = c("CD", "HFD"))
  rownames(coldata) <- coldata$sample_id
  
  dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = cts,
                                colData   = coldata,
                                design    = ~ KO_status + diet)
  dds     <- dds[rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= 3, ]
  vst_obj <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)
  
  cat(genotype, "— samples:", ncol(vst_obj),
      "| genes after filter:", nrow(vst_obj), "\n")
  
  pca_d <- plotPCA(vst_obj, intgroup   = c("KO_status", "diet"),
                   returnData = TRUE,  ntop = 500)
  pv <- round(100 * attr(pca_d, "percentVar"))
  pca_d$sample_id <- rownames(pca_d)
  
  ggplot(pca_d, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                    color = KO_status, shape = diet, label = sample_id)) +
    stat_ellipse(aes(group = KO_status), level = 0.75,
                 linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.95) +
    geom_text_repel(size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 30,
                    box.padding = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("WT" = color_wt, "KO" = color_ko),
                       name   = "Status",
                       labels = c("WT" = paste(genotype, "WT"),
                                  "KO" = paste(genotype, "KO"))) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("CD" = 16, "HFD" = 17), name = "Diet") +
    labs(
      title    = paste("PCA —", genotype, "Samples"),
      subtitle = paste0("Top 500 variable genes  |  PC1: ", pv[1],
                        "%  |  PC2: ", pv[2], "%"),
      x = paste0("PC1 (", pv[1], "% variance)"),
      y = paste0("PC2 (", pv[2], "% variance)")
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
    theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
          legend.position = "right")
}

# ---- Run per genotype ----
p_opa1 <- make_pca_per_group(counts_mat, sample_info_all, "OPA1", "#2166AC", "#92C5DE")
p_atf4 <- make_pca_per_group(counts_mat, sample_info_all, "ATF4", "#1A9641", "#A6D96A")
p_ao   <- make_pca_per_group(counts_mat, sample_info_all, "AO",   "#D73027", "#FDAE61")

# ---- Save individual plots ----
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_OPA1.pdf"), p_opa1, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_OPA1.png"), p_opa1, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 150)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_ATF4.pdf"), p_atf4, width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_ATF4.png"), p_atf4, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 150)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_AO.pdf"),   p_ao,   width = 8, height = 6)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_AO.png"),   p_ao,   width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 150)

# ---- Combined stacked (supplementary) ----
combined <- p_opa1 / p_atf4 / p_ao +
  plot_annotation(
    title   = "PCA — Per Genotype Group",
    caption = "VST-normalized | Top 500 variable genes | Blind = TRUE",
    theme   = theme(plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18, hjust = 0.5),
                    plot.caption = element_text(color = "grey50", size = 9))
  )
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_All_Genotypes_Combined.pdf"), combined, width = 10, height = 18)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_All_Genotypes_Combined.png"), combined, width = 10, height = 18, dpi = 150)

# ---- Combined wide (main figure) ----
combined_wide <- p_opa1 | p_atf4 | p_ao
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_All_Genotypes_Wide.pdf"), combined_wide, width = 24, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir2, "PCA_All_Genotypes_Wide.png"), combined_wide, width = 24, height = 7, dpi = 150)

cat("\n========== ALL OUTPUT FILES ==========\n")
cat("Global PCA saved to:", output_dir, "\n")
cat("  - PCA_all_samples_by_group.pdf/.png\n")
cat("  - PCA_all_samples_by_KO.pdf/.png\n")
cat("  - PCA_all_samples_by_diet.pdf/.png\n")
cat("  - PCA_coordinates.csv\n\n")
cat("Per-genotype PCA saved to:", output_dir2, "\n")
cat("  - PCA_OPA1.pdf/.png\n")
cat("  - PCA_ATF4.pdf/.png\n")
cat("  - PCA_AO.pdf/.png\n")
cat("  - PCA_All_Genotypes_Combined.pdf/.png\n")
cat("  - PCA_All_Genotypes_Wide.pdf/.png\n")

# ============================================================
# STEP 10: PCA — All Samples colored by Genotype Group (AO, ATF4, OPA1)
# ============================================================

genotype_colors <- c(
  "OPA1" = "#2166AC",   # blue
  "ATF4" = "#1A9641",   # green
  "AO"   = "#D73027"    # red
)

p_pca_genotype <- ggplot(pca_data,
                         aes(x = PC1, y = PC2,
                             color = genotype_gene,
                             shape = diet,
                             label = sample_id)) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = genotype_gene), level = 0.75,
               linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.95) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 2.5, max.overlaps = 30,
                  box.padding = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = genotype_colors, name = "Genotype") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("CD" = 16, "HFD" = 17), name = "Diet") +
  labs(
    title    = "PCA — All Samples by Genotype Group",
    subtitle = paste0("Top 500 variable genes | PC1: ", percent_var[1],
                      "% | PC2: ", percent_var[2], "%"),
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percent_var[1], "% variance)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percent_var[2], "% variance)")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40", size = 10),
        legend.position = "right")

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_genotype.pdf"),
       p_pca_genotype, width = 10, height = 7)
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "PCA_all_samples_by_genotype.png"),
       p_pca_genotype, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 150)

cat("Plot saved: PCA_all_samples_by_genotype\n")


```
